summary: Extended the Coolify lane to publish wildcard app DNS from repo-managed edge aliases, created the missing `*.apps.lv3.org` Hetzner record, and verified public resolution plus HTTPS reachability for `education-wemeshup.apps.lv3.org`.

verification.check: Generator, playbook, and repo-governance validation slice
verification.observed: `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml python -m pytest tests/test_generate_platform_vars.py tests/test_coolify_playbook.py tests/test_coolify_tool.py tests/test_lv3_cli.py -q` passed with `71 passed in 3.60s`; `./scripts/validate_repo.sh agent-standards` passed; and `uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python scripts/validate_repository_data_models.py --validate` reported `Repository data models OK`.

`ansible-playbook -i inventory/hosts.yml playbooks/coolify.yml --limit proxmox_hosts --start-at-task 'Ensure Hetzner DNS records are present' --private-key /Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/proxmox_florin_server/.local/ssh/hetzner_llm_agents_ed25519 -e proxmox_guest_ssh_connection_mode=proxmox_host_jump` completed with `proxmox_florin ok=163 changed=0 failed=0`, confirming the Coolify DNS stage now converges cleanly through repo automation.

verification.check: Hetzner authoritative and public recursive DNS resolution
verification.observed: `dig +noall +answer @ns1.your-server.de education-wemeshup.apps.lv3.org A`, `dig +short @8.8.8.8 education-wemeshup.apps.lv3.org A`, and `dig +short @9.9.9.9 education-wemeshup.apps.lv3.org A` all returned `65.108.75.123` after wildcard publication.

verification.check: End-to-end HTTPS reachability through the wildcard lane
verification.observed: `curl -ksSI --resolve education-wemeshup.apps.lv3.org:443:65.108.75.123 https://education-wemeshup.apps.lv3.org/` returned `HTTP/2 200`, and `curl -ks --resolve education-wemeshup.apps.lv3.org:443:65.108.75.123 https://education-wemeshup.apps.lv3.org/api/v1/catalog/taxonomy` returned the production taxonomy payload with API version `v1`, `2` categories, and `6` activities.

notes: The initial scoped create attempt hit an opaque Hetzner API failure while the Ansible DNS role still had `no_log: true` on record creation. Replaying the exact `*.apps` POST directly against the Hetzner DNS API succeeded immediately, and the repo-managed playbook then reran cleanly with no changes.

Public recursive resolvers updated during the same turn, but the controller's default resolver briefly kept the earlier NXDOMAIN cached. A browser may still need a short wait or a local DNS cache refresh after seeing the previous negative answer.
Because the Coolify app lane now owns `*.apps.lv3.org`, individual app create and delete events no longer need one-off DNS record lifecycle work while the wildcard ingress lane remains active.
